text.title: IaC Module Composition Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `IaC Module Composition Patterns`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Root modules MUST only compose leaf modules + pass variables; root modules MUST NOT contain resource declarations directly.
rule.rationale: Mixing resources into root breaks reuse and tangles state files; clear root/leaf split makes refactor mechanical.

rule.statement: Module dependency graph MUST be acyclic; resolving a cycle by moving the offending resource down is required.
rule.rationale: Cyclic refs produce nondeterministic plans + intermittent apply failures.

rule.statement: Module outputs MUST be a stable, documented contract; renaming outputs without major-version bump is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Output renames break every downstream consumer silently on terraform plan.

rule.statement: Each piece of infra state MUST be owned by exactly one module; duplicate ownership across modules is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Duplicate ownership means alternate applies overwrite each other state.

rule.statement: Modules MUST NOT use terraform_remote_state to read across teams without an explicit, versioned interface module.
rule.rationale: Cross-state reads create implicit, untyped contracts that break silently.

rule.statement: If Applies If preconditions fail OR any Skip If trigger fires, do not run this methodology.
